const.eligibility: only normalized identity is supported; every other nonempty valid token sequence is valid-but-unsupported metadata
const.malformed_rule: empty token or invalid token grammar is HEADER_STRUCTURE_INVALID
const.raw_value: first occurrence after SP/HTAB trimming; null when absent
const.repeated_fields: split comma-separated tokens in occurrence order, ASCII lowercase, trim OWS, collapse duplicates preserving first occurrence order

const.charset: collect every unquoted charset token; ASCII lowercase; utf8 aliases to utf-8; identical values collapse; differing values are HEADER_CHARSET_CONFLICT
const.media_type: exact token/token grammar, ASCII lowercase; application/json and application/*+json are supported
const.parameters: after parsing the media-type segment, scan the remaining raw parameter bytes; if any DQUOTE (0x22) or backslash (0x5c) occurs, stop parameter parsing and return HEADER_QUOTED_PARAMETER_UNSUPPORTED. Otherwise split on every semicolon octet, trim only SP/HTAB, and require every nonempty parameter segment to contain exactly one equals sign with token-grammar name and token-grammar value. An empty segment/name/value, multiple equals signs, or non-token byte is HEADER_STRUCTURE_INVALID. Duplicate non-charset parameter names must have identical lowercase-name and exact token-value pairs or HEADER_CONTENT_TYPE_CONFLICT.
const.present_eligibility: media type supported and charset absent or utf-8; empty or other media/charset is valid-but-unsupported
const.quoted_parameter_rule: if any parameter value contains DQUOTE or backslash, metadata is valid-but-unsupported with HEADER_QUOTED_PARAMETER_UNSUPPORTED; no quoted-string or escape parser exists
const.raw_value: first occurrence after SP/HTAB trimming; null only when no Content-Type field occurs; an empty present field remains empty string
const.repeated_fields: parse every occurrence; all normalized media type, parameter multiset, and charset results must be equivalent; otherwise HEADER_CONTENT_TYPE_CONFLICT

const.malformed_field: missing colon or invalid field name is HEADER_STRUCTURE_INVALID
const.obs_fold: any line beginning SP or HTAB is HEADER_OBS_FOLD_REJECTED
const.separator: first colon separates name and value
const.value_trimming: remove only leading and trailing SP and HTAB

const.byte_domain: status and header syntax is ASCII; field values are retained as bytes and decoded ISO-8859-1 one-to-one only for raw persistence and ASCII metadata parsing
const.line_ending_rule: the complete header byte stream must use one accepted delimiter consistently; bare CR or mixed CRLF/LF is HEADER_STRUCTURE_INVALID
const.oversize_rule: when capture reports header_oversize=true, C01 wins and the parser receives no truncated prefix
const.source: exact bytes written by curl --dump-header for one selected completed attempt plus the exact captured body bytes

const.ascii_decode_rule: decode exact bytes as UTF-8; success is guaranteed by the byte domain; JSON success/failure is evaluated later and separately
const.bom_decode_rule: remove exactly one leading EF BB BF and decode the remainder as strict UTF-8; JSON success/failure is evaluated later and separately
evaluation_order: if body starts EF BB BF, validate all remaining bytes as strict UTF-8; valid is NO_CONTENT_TYPE_BODY_UTF8_BOM_VALID, invalid is NO_CONTENT_TYPE_BODY_UTF8_BOM_INVALID
else if every body byte is 00..7F, including empty body, state is NO_CONTENT_TYPE_BODY_ASCII
const.unsupported_rule: map conservatively to C14 without current-body JSON parsing

const.HEADER_CONTENT_TYPE_ABSENT_BODY_DEPENDEN: selected HTTP/1.1 block is structurally valid, Content-Type is absent, and eligibility is decided only by the registered body-byte domain
const.HEADER_METADATA_ELIGIBLE: selected HTTP/1.1 block is structurally valid, present Content-Type metadata is supported, and Content-Encoding is identity
const.HEADER_METADATA_UNSUPPORTED: selected HTTP/1.1 block is structurally valid but present metadata or absent-header body domain is outside supported contract; C14
const.HEADER_PROTOCOL_POLICY_VIOLATION: selected block has a recognized non-HTTP/1.1 protocol; structural protocol stop before compatibility body analysis
const.HEADER_STRUCTURE_INVALID: captured header structure is malformed or ambiguous; request-level C14 unless the extractor output/capture binding is schema-invalid

const.analysis_gate: selected_http_protocol must equal HTTP/1.1 before compatibility body analysis; otherwise STOP_HTTP_PROTOCOL_POLICY_VIOLATION

const.block_rule: each nonempty block begins with exactly one recognized status line; any other first line is HEADER_STATUS_LINE_INVALID

const.rule: execute exact registered header and body bytes once in order and compare every expected field; no substitute corpus
